⇗ Top

Price: ~$337.11 | Entry Zone: $No clear entry zone visible at current price; significant pullback needed.

Technicals

  • The stock is in a strong uptrend, but has recently experienced a parabolic move, breaking significantly above its established upward channel.
  • The Relative Strength Index (RSI) is at 80.02, indicating highly overbought conditions. The purple RSI line is above the orange signal line, both well into the overbought territory.
  • A 'Sell' signal dot is visible on the chart near the current price level, suggesting potential downward pressure.
  • Previous 'Buy' signals were observed at much lower price levels (e.g., around $180-$200 and $210-$220), indicating the current price is far from recent accumulation zones.

News & Financials

  • No news or financial data is visible on the provided chart to inform the analysis.

Assessment

  • Buy level: A significant pullback to previous support levels (e.g., $220-$250 range) or a period of consolidation with the RSI cooling off below the overbought threshold would be required for a potential buy consideration.
  • Risk: High risk due to the stock being significantly overbought, trading far above its established trend channel, and the presence of a 'Sell' signal. A correction or consolidation is highly probable.
  • Action: Given the current portfolio allocation of 3.5% (40 shares) and the high-risk, overbought technical conditions, it is recommended to hold the existing position but strongly advise against adding more shares at this time. Consider reducing exposure if the price shows signs of reversal.

DGRO iShares Core Dividend Growth ETF (~$72.71) WAIT

⇗ Top

Price: ~$72.71 | Entry Zone: $70.00–71.50

Technicals

  • RSI purple line has crossed above the orange signal line, indicating positive momentum.
  • A 'Buy' signal was recently triggered near the $70.00 support level in late April.
  • Price is currently recovering from a recent dip but is approaching a local resistance zone near $73.00-$74.00.
  • The long-term trend structure remains bullish with a series of higher lows visible on the daily chart.

News & Financials

  • DGRO tracks US stocks with at least five years of consecutive dividend growth, offering a balance of yield and capital appreciation.
  • Low expense ratio of 0.08% makes it an efficient core holding for long-term portfolios.
  • The ETF is well-diversified across sectors, reducing idiosyncratic risk compared to individual stocks.

Assessment

  • Buy level: Wait for a minor pullback to the $71.00 level or a confirmed breakout above the previous high of $74.50.
  • Risk: Sensitivity to interest rate changes and broader market volatility affecting large-cap dividend payers.
  • Action: With a current allocation of 1.8%, the position is modest. Recommend holding current shares and waiting for a better entry point closer to $71.00 before increasing the allocation to a larger core position.

  • The RSI purple line is currently at 33.30, trending below the orange signal line, indicating bearish momentum.

  • The oscillator recently bounced from extreme oversold conditions (sub-20) but has not yet confirmed a bullish crossover.

News & Financials

  • Gold prices are highly sensitive to US Dollar strength and Treasury yield fluctuations.
  • Ongoing geopolitical uncertainty typically provides a floor for gold as a safe-haven asset.
  • Market expectations for interest rate cuts generally act as a tailwind for non-yielding assets like GLD.

Assessment

  • Buy level: Wait for a confirmed RSI crossover above the orange signal line or a successful retest of the $400 support level.
  • Risk: Breakdown below the $400 psychological support could lead to a deeper correction toward the $380 zone.
  • Action: Hold current 2.7% allocation. Do not add more shares until the RSI confirms a bullish trend reversal to avoid catching a falling knife.
